title: BACKND Base federation login and account linking
summary: Provides a narrow routing layer for federation login, signup, and migration questions so agents can move from the broad user-auth area to the exact official federation pages before answering mutable provider details.
capabilities: route provider-specific login questions to federation pages
distinguish federation signup or login from account migration
highlight that provider support and permissions are mutable upstream facts
identify when the answer requires official-doc verification before naming providers or setup steps
prerequisites: A provider-issued token or provider login flow is available before federation auth is attempted
The game account strategy allows federation login or migration
common_pitfalls: Storing provider support lists in the pack as if they were immutable facts
Answering provider-specific setup questions from the umbrella user-auth topic without checking the federation routes
Confusing federation login, signup, and migration as the same flow
verification_strategy: Use https://docs.backnd.com plus the source route before listing current federation providers or provider-specific settings.
Do not answer provider names from cached pack content alone when freshness matters.
public_notes: This is intentionally a thin topic: it improves routing accuracy without storing a mutable provider matrix.
Agents should verify current provider support from the official federation page before answering exact platform lists.
